name: Beardstown Savings S.B.
description_short: Mutual savings bank founded in 1880 serving Cass County, Illinois with checking, savings, lending, and investment products across two locations.
description_long:
Beardstown Savings, S.B. is a mutually owned financial institution established in 1880, making it one of the oldest banks in Cass County, Illinois. As a mutual bank, it is owned by its depositors rather than shareholders, allowing the institution to prioritize customer service over profit maximization. The bank operates two physical locations in Beardstown and Mt. Sterling, Illinois.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of deposit and lending products. On the deposit side, customers can access checking accounts, savings accounts, money market deposit accounts (MMDAs), certificates of deposit (CDs), and individual retirement accounts (IRAs). The lending division provides auto loans, consumer and personal loans, mortgage lending (including first-time homebuyer programs), agricultural lending, and commercial lending. Additional services include mobile and online banking, check ordering, and bill payment capabilities. All deposits are FDIC-insured, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

What distinguishes Beardstown Savings is its mutual ownership structure and deep community roots. The bank explicitly markets itself as the only mutually owned financial institution in the tri-county area, emphasizing customer-first philosophy rather than shareholder returns. The institution has received an Outstanding CRA (Community Reinvestment Act) rating, demonstrating a commitment to community lending. The bank's tagline "Bank Different" emphasizes its alternative approach to traditional banking.

However, potential customers should note that this is a small regional bank with only two physical locations in rural Illinois. Online banking and mobile capabilities exist but may be more limited than those offered by larger national banks. The fee structure includes standard charges ($25 overdraft fees, $25 NSF fees, $25 stop payment fees) and monthly dormant account fees of $2.00. As a small institution, Beardstown Savings may have fewer product customization options and lower deposit insurance limits awareness compared to larger competitors.
services: Mortgage lending with first-time homebuyer programs
pros: Mutually owned structure means profits are returned to customers rather than external shareholders
Oldest bank in Cass County (founded 1880) with 140+ years of community banking history
Outstanding CRA rating demonstrates strong community lending commitment
FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Offers first-time homebuyer programs and home renovation financing options
Two convenient branch locations in Beardstown and Mt. Sterling
Free notary services for customers and free faxing for customers
cons: Limited to two physical locations in rural Illinois with no nationwide presence or ATM network mentioned
Small institution may lack advanced digital banking features compared to national banks
$25 overdraft and NSF fees are standard but may be burdensome for budget-conscious consumers
$2.00/month dormant account fee could accumulate for inactive accounts
Agricultural and commercial lending focus suggests possible bias toward business customers over retail consumers
best_for: Residents of Beardstown or Mt. Sterling, Illinois seeking local community banking relationships
First-time homebuyers interested in specialized programs and personalized mortgage guidance
Customers prioritizing mutual ownership and customer-first banking philosophy over corporate returns
Agricultural and small business owners in the tri-county area needing specialized lending
diagnosis: Beardstown Savings is best for individuals and businesses in rural central Illinois (Cass County area) seeking community-focused banking with a mutual ownership structure and strong local roots. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this is exclusively a regional bank with only two branches, making it impractical for customers outside the immediate service area or those requiring nationwide banking access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
